DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE **IF IT IS DISCOVERED THROUGH FORMAL OR INFORMAL DISCOVERY THAT DEFENDANT ALPHABET INC. IS AN APPROPRIATE PARTY TO THIS ACTION, ALPHABET INC. AGREES TO TOLL THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND WAIVE LACHES DEFENSES; WITH THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS TOLLED BY ONE YEAR FROM THE ORIGINAL DEADLINE ON OR AROUND FEBRUARY 16, 2027, TO FEBRUARY 16, 2028; AND ALPHABET INC. AGREES NOT TO PURSUE PLAINTIFF FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION BASED ON THE MATTERS ALLEGED IN THIS LAWSUIT** (TRANSACTION ID # 76918174) AS TO DEFENDANT ALPHABET INC.

Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Aria Noosha, Esq. 415-645-4510 Insta Law for Plaintiff.
Paul Nystrom, Esq. 248-203-0855ykema for Defendant.
Court reporter: Vienna Nguyen, CSR #13137, 949-295-5737, viennanguyen@gmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr. Noosha presents arguments; stands on court's TR, overbroad, motion to be quashed. Mr. Nystrom presents arguments; motion should be denied. Court comments; seriously meet and confer, have an open mind, good faith effort, discovery act is self executed, resolve disputes amongst yourselves, negotiiated solution better than an imposed one by the court.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Jennifer Hanki's Motion to Quash Subpoenas.

Taken Under Submission.***

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENIFER HANKI DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Jenifer Hanki's demurrer to the answer filed by defendants Waymo LLC is overruled in its entirety.

The demurrer serves no useful purpose. The affirmative defenses alleged in Waymo's answer are commonly alleged in a personal injury case. While it is likely that many, if not most, of the affirmative defenses will be withdrawn before trial, at this early stage of the case it is neither improper nor outside the norms of Bay Area litigation for Waymo to have alleged them. If Ms. Hanki believes she needs more information about the affirmative defenses, she may propound discovery seeking that information.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
